teaching.concept: A compact workplace change note separates preparation, ordered steps, what must stay safe, completion checks, and a record.
teaching.howTo: Use five H2 sections, short lists, one blockquote, and one fenced text record.
teaching.example:
# Meeting room name change

Rename one shared room while the old sign remains visible for a short handoff.

## Before

- Record the current bookings

candidates.title: Structure an API-field deprecation
candidates.prompt: Rebuild a compact meeting-room change note with preparation, ordered steps, one safety note, completion checks, and a record.
candidates.target:
# Meeting room name change

Rename one shared room while the old sign remains visible for a short handoff.

## Before

- Record the current bookings
- List teams using the room

## Steps

1. Post the new room name
2. Update future bookings
3. Remove the old sign

## Keep

> Existing bookings must keep their original times.

## Done when

- New notices use one room name
- Reception has the updated map

## Record

```text
Owner: Office team | Status: confirmed
```

# Supply cabinet relabeling

Move shared supplies to clearer shelves without interrupting the morning shift.

- Photograph the current shelves
- Count frequently used items

## Done when

- Every shelf has one label
- The stock sheet matches the cabinet

## Record

```text
Owner: Facilities | Status: checked
```

# Duplicate supply request issue

Trace why one paper request can appear twice on the weekly order sheet.

## Evidence

> Request SR-18 appeared in two rows after one handoff.

---

## Reproduce

1. Open the order sheet
2. Copy the afternoon notes
3. Review request `SR-18`

## What changes

- Keep one sheet owner
- Preserve the original request date

## Verification

- Compare label `SR-18`
- Confirm status `ready`

Replace the legacy adapter while preserving event names, consent, and payload allowlists.

## Files

- Edit `adapter.ts`

candidates.prompt: Rebuild a compact developer refactor brief for replacing an analytics adapter.
candidates.target:
# Analytics adapter refactor brief

Replace the legacy adapter while preserving event names, consent, and payload allowlists.

## Files

- Edit `adapter.ts`
- Edit `adapter.test.ts`

## Refactor

1. Pin event fixtures
2. Add the approved client
3. Move verified callers

## Guardrails

- Keep raw customer text out
- Do not bypass consent

## Verify

```bash
npm test --workspace packages/analytics
```

## Report

- **Changed:** adapter boundary
- **Checked:** consent and payloads

# API contract rollout brief

Move one versioned response from the provider to supported consumers with a pause rule.

## System map

- API provider

candidates.title: Write a coordinated API contract rollout order
candidates.prompt: Rebuild a compact developer rollout brief for one versioned API response.
candidates.target:
# API contract rollout brief

Move one versioned response from the provider to supported consumers with a pause rule.

## System map

- API provider
  - Versioned schema
  - Compatibility adapter
- Web and mobile consumers

## Rollout

1. Publish the compatible response
2. Move supported consumers
3. Remove the old adapter

## Pause

> Pause when one supported consumer cannot read the response.

## Checks

- Run `npm test --workspace apps/api`
- Run `npm test --workspace apps/web`

## Report

```markdown
# API rollout report
- Result:
- Evidence:
```

# Session verification migration brief

Move verification behind the approved signed-session adapter without changing login behavior.

## Read first

1. Read [agent rules](/AGENTS.md)
2. Read the signed-session decision

## Scope

- Edit `sessionVerifier.ts`
- Preserve account identifiers

1. Pin current session fixtures
2. Add the signed verifier
3. Test the login flows

## Stop

> Stop if rollback would invalidate every active session.

## Verify

```bash
npm test --workspace apps/auth -- sessions
```

## Report

- **Changed:** verifier boundary
- **Checked:** login and rollback

Restore one idempotent worker path while preserving delivery evidence.

> One queue message produced the same delivery identifier twice.

---


- [Recovery note](/reports/job-recovery)

# Report performance recovery brief

Improve one measured report path while preserving authorization, order, and export fields.

## Read first

1. Read [agent rules](/AGENTS.md)
2. Read the performance budget

## Scope

- Edit `monthlyReport.ts`
- Use the existing benchmark fixture

## Plan

1. Record a stable baseline
2. Make one bounded change
3. Repeat the same benchmark

## Stop

> Stop if the baseline cannot reproduce the slow path.

## Verify

```bash
npm run benchmark -- monthly-report
```

## Report

- **Changed:** measured query path
- **Checked:** order and authorization

candidates.title: Write a bounded release-context work order
candidates.prompt: Rebuild a compact developer brief for restoring shared release context.
candidates.target:
# Shared release context brief

Restore the approved release context from storage without fetching it during requests.

## Read first

1. Read [agent rules](/AGENTS.md)
2. Read the storage decision

## Scope

- Edit `releaseContext.ts`
- Preserve the public response type

## Plan

1. Pin the context contract
2. Add idempotent storage
3. Move the request reader

## Stop

> Stop if migration history cannot be reconciled safely.

## Verify

```bash
npm test --workspace apps/api -- release-context
```

## Report

- **Changed:** storage and reader
- **Checked:** request regression

teaching.concept: A recovery brief keeps evidence, repair order, constraints, proof, and reporting visible without becoming an incident report.
teaching.howTo: Use quoted evidence, one divider, three numbered actions, short constraints, a command fence, and a linked report item.
teaching.example:
# Search index recovery brief

Repair stale facets while the primary database remains the source of truth.

## Evidence

> One record has different categories in the database and index.

candidates.title: Write a search index recovery work order
candidates.prompt: Rebuild a compact developer recovery brief for a stale search index.
candidates.target:
# Search index recovery brief

Repair stale facets while the primary database remains the source of truth.

## Evidence

> One record has different categories in the database and index.

---

## Recovery

1. Capture one mismatch in a fixture
2. Update the index mapping
3. Rebuild only the checked records

## Constraints

- Keep primary records unchanged
- Do not invent missing results

## Verify

```bash
npm test --workspace apps/search -- consistency
```

## Report

- **Result:** consistent facets
- [Recovery note](/reports/index-recovery)
